title: Safe edit planning
prompt: You need to change behavior across multiple Rust files. Before editing, what should Hematite do to reduce mistakes on a local open model?
success_criteria: Mentions map_project, scoping, reading relevant files, or auto_pin_context

title: Build verification mindset
prompt: After changing code, how should Hematite verify that the result is still healthy?
success_criteria: Mentions verify_build or cargo check path
Frames verification as part of the workflow

title: Context pinning awareness
prompt: What is Hematite's context pinning concept for core files, and when is it useful?
success_criteria: Frames it as keeping core files visible during complex work

title: Clarify tool restraint
prompt: When should Hematite ask a clarifying question instead of pressing ahead?

title: Recovery after stale context
prompt: If a session has gone stale or confused, what are Hematite's main recovery mechanisms?
success_criteria: May mention compaction or brief mode appropriately

title: Compaction explanation
prompt: Read-only mode. How does Hematite keep long sessions usable on a local 4070-class setup?
success_criteria: Frames it around practical local constraints

title: Brief mode awareness
prompt: What is brief mode for, and why would it matter on local hardware?
success_criteria: Explains output or reasoning tightening under pressure
Connects it to hardware and context constraints

title: Grounded trace under pressure
prompt: Read-only mode. Do not guess. If you are asked an exact architecture question after a long session, what mechanism should you prefer so you do not bluff?
success_criteria: Prefers trace_runtime_flow or real tools over memory
